resources.string: Explore seasonal fruit trees around Seattle.
A Seattle fruit map with a small story.
Discover neighborhood fruit trees, revisit them in season, and share local finds responsibly.

Zogal started with a simple discovery.

After moving to Seattle, I missed a fruit from home — cornelian cherry, known as dogwood cherry or “zoğal” in Azerbaijan. I never expected to find it growing in a neighborhood tree, but one day I did, and it felt like reconnecting with a small piece of home.

That moment inspired this app.

Zogal helps people discover fruit trees around Seattle, learn about unfamiliar fruit, and share local finds with others. Add trees you know, explore nearby spots on the map, and uncover hidden seasonal fruit in your city.

Please forage responsibly: plants and fruits can be difficult to identify, and some may be unsafe to eat. When in doubt, avoid tasting and always verify identification first. Nature belongs to wildlife too — enjoy respectfully and sustainably.
Go discover your first fruit tree.
Long-press on the map to pin a tree
Zogal does not guarantee plant safety or identification accuracy. Never consume plants unless properly identified by an expert.
Privacy Policy: Zogal stores your profile, saved trees, photos, and reports only to support community discovery features. Do not upload private locations you do not want associated with your account.
Terms of Service: Use Zogal as a discovery map, not as an edible safety authority. You are responsible for respecting property, local rules, wildlife, and safe identification.
